comment: Demo #4 refund agent — DECLARATIVE Colmena DAG. trigger -> draft(llm) -> validate(rule-based policy, cyclic retry) -> confirm(llm agent: get_key=secure_suspend tool + pay=secure python tool) -> review(suspend HITL) -> decide(router) -> log per branch. Customer prompt injected via inject_payload {"prompt": ...}; ${OPENAI_API_KEY}/${DATABASE_URL} resolve from env. Two suspends still fire in sequence so the runner resumes twice: (1) the `confirm` agent calls its get_key tool (secure_suspend) which SUSPENDS for the payment key — resumed with A[pay_key]: <secret>; (2) the human approval `review` — resumed with A[approve_refund]: <answer>. OUTBOUND-MASKING (the whole point of the demo): the secret-bearing payment runs as a `secure:true` TOOL inside `confirm`'s llm_call tool loop. get_key returns the key only as a masked HANDLE (<sv_...>); the agent passes that handle to `pay`; the engine decrypts it ONLY to run the python tool, then DagToolExecutor RE-MASKS the tool result (auth_token -> <value_N>) BEFORE it re-enters the LLM conversation. So the secret genuinely flows through an LLM yet never reaches the model/proxy in the clear. WHY a tool (not a plain edge): a plain DAG edge into an llm_call's prompt does NOT mask — run_use_case.rs re-injects (decrypts) secure handles into every non-`llm` node's inputs, and the LLM node is registered as `llm_call` (not `llm`), so the inject-skip guard misses it and the raw secret would reach the prompt. Only the tool-executor masking pass protects values that re-enter an LLM.

config.system_message: You are a refund support agent. Decide the refund for the customer's order. POLICY (strict): a single agent may auto-approve refunds up to 100 USD; for amounts above 100 USD you MUST choose 'partial' (amount <= 100) or 'escalate'; NEVER auto-approve more than 100 USD. Return ONLY a single JSON object, no prose, no code fences, with exactly these keys: {"decision": "approve|partial|reject|escalate", "amount": <number>, "justification": "<text>"}. If a reviewer left feedback on your previous draft, you MUST correct it: {{feedback}}

You finalize a customer refund. Do EXACTLY these steps in order: (1) Call the `get_key` tool to obtain the payment-gateway API key. It returns the key as an opaque HANDLE that looks like <sv_...> — that handle IS the key, treat it as the secret. (2) Call the `pay` tool, passing the handle returned by get_key EXACTLY as the `pay_key` argument (paste the <sv_...> handle verbatim; never invent a value). (3) Using ONLY the order_info from the pay tool result, write a ONE-LINE customer-facing confirmation that the refund was processed. Do not invent data. NEVER reveal, echo, or repeat the API key / auth_token — if a value looks like <sv_...> or <value_N>, it is a masked secret; do not put it in your final message.

fixed.question: Provide the payment-gateway API key for the refund.
fixed.name: pay_key

pay.name: pay
pay.description: Charge the refund to the payment gateway and return the order info plus the gateway auth token. Pass the payment-gateway API key HANDLE (the <sv_...> value returned by get_key) as pay_key.

pay_key.description: The payment-gateway API key handle (<sv_...>) returned by the get_key tool. Paste it exactly; do not invent a value.

intent.description: approve if the human approves / says yes / authorizes the refund; reject if they decline or cancel; escalate if they ask to escalate or are unsure.
